documentation.readme: A flexible tool that creates a minidump of the LSASS process.
usage_sections: skelsec for writing minidump, which was crucial for learning the minidump file format.
freefirex from CS-Situational-Awareness-BOF at Trustedsec for many cool tricks for BOFs
Antonio Cocomazzi for Abusing leaked handles to dump LSASS memory and Racing for LSASS dumps

arguments.name: --chunk-size
arguments.description: chunk size in KiB used to exfiltrate the dump without touching disk (default 900)

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: duplicate a high privileged existing LSASS handle

arguments.name: -de
arguments.description: duplicate a low privileged existing LSASS handle and then elevate it

arguments.name: --duplicate
arguments.description: duplicate a high privileged existing LSASS handle

arguments.name: --duplicate-elevate
arguments.description: duplicate a low privileged existing LSASS handle and then elevate it

arguments.name: -eh
arguments.description: open a handle to LSASS with low privileges and duplicate it to gain higher privileges

arguments.name: --elevate-handle
arguments.description: open a handle to LSASS with low privileges and duplicate it to gain higher privileges

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: fork the target process before dumping

arguments.name: --fork
arguments.description: fork the target process before dumping

arguments.name: --getpid
arguments.description: print the PID of LSASS and leave

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: print this help message and leave

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: print this help message and leave

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: snapshot the target process before dumping

arguments.name: -sc
arguments.description: open a handle to LSASS using a fake calling stack

arguments.name: -sd
arguments.description: make seclogon open a handle to LSASS and duplicate it

arguments.name: --seclogon-duplicate
arguments.description: make seclogon open a handle to LSASS and duplicate it

arguments.name: --seclogon-leak-local
arguments.description: leak an LSASS handle into nanodump via seclogon

arguments.name: --shtinkering
arguments.description: force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via Shtinkering

arguments.name: -sk
arguments.description: force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via Shtinkering

arguments.name: -sll
arguments.description: leak an LSASS handle into nanodump via seclogon

arguments.name: --snapshot
arguments.description: snapshot the target process before dumping

arguments.name: --spoof-callstack
arguments.description: open a handle to LSASS using a fake calling stack

arguments.name: -theft
arguments.description: a hit after a hit-and-miss start](https://www.matteomalvica.com/blog/2019/12/02/win-defender-atp-cred-bypass/)

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: create a dump with a valid signature

arguments.name: --valid
arguments.description: create a dump with a valid signature

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP/SOCKS proxy URL.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header (key:value).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output (machine-readable).

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

examples: Let WerFault.exe (instead of nanodump) create the dump
force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via SilentProcessExit
force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via Shtinkering
If you prefer to stay on linux, you can use the python3 port of mimikatz called [pypykatz]($KFIOSA_TARGET_URL
Dump LSASS bypassing PPL using the PPLDump exploit, duplicating the handle that csrss.exe has on LSASS:
Make the WerFault.exe process create a full memory dump in the Temp folder:

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: A flexible tool that creates a minidump of the LSASS process.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A flexible tool that creates a minidump of the LSASS process.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/fortra/nanodump.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Let WerFault.exe (instead of nanodump) create the dump`. Further invocations from upstream: `force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via SilentProcessExit`; `force WerFault.exe to dump LSASS via Shtinkering`. A flexible tool that creates a minidump of the LSASS process.

name: nanodump
summary: A flexible tool that creates a minidump of the LSASS process.
